terms.definition: The degree to which parts of a sociocultural system act in mutually reinforcing ways
The public performance of unanimous moral virtue in late-19th/early-20th-century America
The tendency of Progressive moral reforms to build central state capacity that later undermines localist values
Combined rate of desertion, separation, and divorce per married population
Economic activity oriented toward household survival rather than surplus accumulation
A Protestant movement locating salvation in the reform of social structures
State-mandated minimum schooling enforced by attendance laws
Process by which diverse organizations adopt similar structures under shared pressures
Collective extra-judicial killing used to enforce racial hierarchy
Federal statutes (1873) banning obscene materials, including contraceptives, from the mails
